words.clue: Only complete cartilaginous ring in the respiratory tract
Largest laryngeal cartilage with two laminae and the laryngeal prominence
Leaf-shaped elastic cartilage that folds down during swallowing to protect the airway
Pyramid-shaped paired cartilage with vocal and muscular processes
The only muscle that abducts the vocal cords
Nerve whose injury during thyroid surgery causes hoarseness
Membrane pierced for emergency airway access below the vocal cords
Space between the true vocal cords and arytenoid cartilages
Laryngeal compartment with the richest lymphatic drainage
Type of carcinoma most commonly affecting the larynx
